topics.title: Ketamine Effects on Brain Connectivity and Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on ketamine administration and its dose-dependent effects on neural information dynamics, connectivity, and perception in healthy adults and major depression. The abstracts emphasize connectome characterization, brain network modeling, and markers of therapeutic response.

keywords: functional mapping of cerebellothalamic networks after focused ultrasound
topics.title: Focused Ultrasound Effects in Essential Tremor
topics.description: Studies of focused ultrasound thalamotomy for essential tremor, especially Vim-targeted procedures, examine motor improvement and durable outcomes. The cluster emphasizes cerebellothalamic network mapping, connectivity fingerprints, and longitudinal functional and structural reorganization after treatment.

keywords: traumatic brain injury and post traumatic
topics.title: PTSD Neuroimaging and Biomarker Signatures
topics.description: This cluster focuses on PTSD and related trauma conditions, including combat-related, comorbid, and post-traumatic stress following traumatic brain injury. The studies emphasize neuroimaging, connectivity, white matter, amygdala, and neurotransmitter-related signatures that may distinguish symptom profiles and biomarkers.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms and Phenotypes of Chronic Pain
topics.description: This cluster centers on how chronic pain is represented in the brain, including morphometric, connectivity, and network-level signatures across conditions such as fibromyalgia, migraine, and trigeminal neuralgia. It also highlights mechanisms like central sensitization, pain perception, and fear-avoidance processes.

keywords: cerebellar structural networks link dual physical cognitive impairment
topics.title: Cerebellar Network Changes and Cognitive Impairment
topics.description: This cluster centers on cerebellar structural and diffusion MRI findings in stroke, ataxia, and aging, with emphasis on cortico-cerebellar pathways and network topology. It also highlights links between cerebellar changes and cognitive or dual physical-cognitive impairment in older adults.

keywords: functional neural predictors of early alcohol initiation in emerging adolescence
topics.title: Neuroimaging Predictors of Adolescent Substance Use
topics.description: This cluster centers on how adolescent brain development and functional neural predictors relate to early alcohol initiation, nicotine use, and broader substance use trajectories. It emphasizes neuroimaging biomarkers, developmental trajectory, and family history in predicting substance use outcomes during emerging adolescence.

keywords: functional connectivity predict crave across substance use disorder
ventral striatum connectivity predicts craving and abstinence
topics.title: Craving and Connectivity in Substance Use Disorders
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how drug cues, craving, and abstinence are linked to fronto-striatal and ventral striatal network connectivity across substance use disorders. It emphasizes transdiagnostic neuroimaging findings relevant to reward circuitry and treatment outcome.

topics.title: Neuroimaging Biomarkers of Alcohol Risk
topics.description: This cluster centers on alcohol use disorder and related risk states, with emphasis on comorbid social anxiety disorder, inhibitory control, and brain autonomic integration. Studies use multimodal neuroimaging data and functional connectivity measures to support biomarker discovery and prediction.

keywords: bimanual vr mirror therapy modulates sensorimotor network activation after stroke
cortical oscillations reflect adaptive updating of internal reach
topics.title: Motor Network Plasticity After Stroke
topics.description: This cluster centers on sensorimotor and action-observation network reorganization in stroke and related motor rehabilitation contexts. It also includes studies linking cortical oscillations, EEG, and high-field fMRI to motor control, body representation, and adaptive updating.

keywords: lsd reshapes cortical dynamics through peak frequency shifts
topics.title: Psychedelic Effects on Brain Network Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how psychedelics such as psilocybin and LSD alter brain activity, functional connectivity, and large-scale cortical organization. It also includes analytic approaches for characterizing these changes, such as dynamic causal model and eeg microstate analysis.

topics.title: Brain Network Changes After Traumatic Injury
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how traumatic brain injury, concussion, and repeated head impacts alter brain structure and connectivity across development and recovery. Studies use multimodal neuroimaging and network analyses to characterize microstructural and cortical changes associated with cognitive outcomes.

keywords: functional network remapping following brain tumor surgery
topics.title: Network Reorganization After Brain Tumor Surgery
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how brain tumors and their surgical treatment alter functional and structural networks, with attention to glioma-related connectivity changes and lesion effects. It also emphasizes predicting cognitive and functional outcomes after surgery, including postoperative deficits and recovery.

topics.title: Olfactory Processing and Body Odor Perception
topics.description: This cluster centers on how smell and body odor are processed in the brain, including identification, brain response, and experience-dependent modulation. It also includes olfactory dysfunction, congenital anosmia, and related neural mechanisms in patients.

keywords: long term aerobic exercise reconfigures directed resting state eeg network topology
topics.title: Exercise Effects on Brain Connectivity and White Matter
topics.description: This cluster centers on how exercise and aerobic training relate to brain network organization, functional connectivity, and white matter microstructure across the adult lifespan. It also includes differences by intensity, sex, and fitness gains in healthy and sedentary adults.

topics.title: Stroke Lesions and Recovery Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on how stroke and focal brain lesion characteristics relate to functional recovery, motor control, and broader clinical outcomes. It also emphasizes predictive approaches using connectivity, tract-based measures, and lesion mapping to explain recovery and severity.

topics.title: Connectomic Deep Brain Stimulation Outcomes
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep brain stimulation across movement and neuropsychiatric disorders, with emphasis on Parkinson disease, dystonia, Tourette syndrome, and refractory epilepsy. The abstracts focus on connectomic and network-based approaches to predicting, personalizing, and interpreting stimulation outcomes.

topics.title: Epilepsy Localization and Imaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on epilepsy subtypes and the localization of epileptogenic tissue using invasive and noninvasive recordings. It also includes imaging and pediatric or autoimmune-related contexts that inform diagnosis and surgical planning.

keywords: peritumoral diffusion properties indicate latent glioma subtypes
longitudinal tract specific diffusion spectroscopic change
ultra low field diffusion weight mri
topics.title: Diffusion Imaging of Glioma and White Matter
topics.description: These studies use advanced diffusion MRI and tractography to characterize glioma-related white matter changes, including peritumoral diffusion properties and latent glioma subtypes. They also emphasize individualized detection and connectome-level analysis of microstructural abnormalities.

keywords: microstates pipeline for source level connectivity and classification in multiple sclerosis
topics.title: Network and Connectivity Markers of Fatigue in MS
topics.description: This cluster centers on multiple sclerosis fatigue, especially cognitive fatigue, and the brain network abnormalities associated with it. Studies emphasize EEG microstates, functional connectivity, multimodal MRI, and iron deposition as potential markers of network localization and reorganization.

topics.title: MRI Biomarkers of Multiple Sclerosis Progression
topics.description: This cluster centers on multiple sclerosis, emphasizing MRI-based measures of myelin, structural brain changes, and lesion evolution. It also links imaging findings with disability, cognitive status, and disease severity over time.

keywords: cerebral perfusion dynamics during and follow exertion
the impact of bioenergetic challenges on functional brain dynamics
topics.title: Bioenergetic Stress and Brain Network Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how acute metabolic and stress-related challenges alter brain function, perfusion, and network organization. It links bioenergetic state to functional integration, deactivation patterns, and neurometabolic responses across cognitive and fatigue-related contexts.

topics.title: Mindfulness and Brain Network Changes
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how mindfulness and related meditation practices alter interoceptive processing, bodily representation, and brain network connectivity. The abstracts emphasize functional and connectivity-based neuroimaging findings linked to mental health and symptom improvement.

topics.title: Cerebellar Development Across the Lifespan
topics.description: This cluster centers on structural and functional development of the cerebellum from birth through early adulthood, including lobular organization, surface area, thickness, and connectivity. It also includes studies of developmental atlases and alterations associated with preterm birth, with links to cytoarchitecture and lifespan trajectories.

topics.title: Brain Structure, Development, and Asymmetry
topics.description: This cluster centers on normative human brain structure and anatomy, including asymmetry and functional gradients across development. It also links cortical structure to epigenetic sensitivity and transcriptomic features underlying individual differences in cognition.

topics.title: Sleep, Arousal, and CSF Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how sleep stages, anesthesia, and arousal transitions shape brain state dynamics and consciousness. It also highlights cerebrospinal fluid and BOLD coupling as a key physiological correlate across these states.

topics.title: Brainstem and MRI changes in REM sleep disorder
topics.description: Studies in this cluster focus on structural brain abnormalities, especially brainstem and white matter changes, in REM sleep behavior disorder and related isolated forms. The abstracts also link MRI morphometry findings with phenoconversion and neuroanatomical correlates such as the hypothalamic nucleus and VII brainstem pathway.

keywords: multimodal imaging signatures of cognitive decline
topics.title: Neurodegenerative Markers of Cognitive Decline
topics.description: This cluster centers on cognitive decline in Parkinson and Lewy body disease, especially prodromal and mild cognitive impairment stages. It highlights multimodal imaging, cholinergic and neuromodulatory pathways, glymphatic dysfunction, and biomarker signatures linked to executive decline.

keywords: white matter microstructure peripheral inflammatory cytokine level
topics.title: White Matter and Clinical Correlates in Psychosis
topics.description: This cluster focuses on white matter microstructure and related imaging measures in schizophrenia and early psychosis, including chr cohorts and fixel-based analysis. It also links these measures with symptom severity, illness duration, sex differences, and peripheral inflammatory cytokine levels.

keywords: variability dependent disruption of structural functional coupling
topics.title: Brain Variability and Anxiety in Adolescents
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how brain signal variability and resting-state functional coupling relate to anxiety, social anxiety disorder, and cognitive function in adolescents. It also highlights sex differences and lateralization effects in brain function and connectivity.

topics.title: Eating Disorders and Obesity Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain network alterations and biomarkers associated with obesity, anorexia nervosa, binge eating disorder, and Prader-Willi syndrome. It emphasizes resting-state connectivity, structure-function coupling, and hypothalamic or white matter changes across developmental and treatment-related contexts.

topics.title: Hallucinations and Connectivity in Parkinsonian Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on visual hallucinations and related minor hallucinations in Parkinsonian and Lewy body disorders, especially in early disease and dementia. It emphasizes connectivity and structural-metabolic alterations, including frontal, thalamo-cortical, and brainstem changes underlying hallucination awareness and progression.

topics.title: White Matter Development Across Infancy
topics.description: This cluster centers on typical white matter development and maturation from infancy through early childhood, including age-related and developmental trajectories. It also highlights tract-specific and longitudinal analyses of microstructural organization in developing brains.

topics.title: Neural and behavioral mechanisms of food choice
topics.description: This cluster centers on how neural correlates, reward processing, and decision-making mechanisms shape food choice and related behaviors such as distracted eating, habitual behavior, and risk-sensitive choice. It also includes work on computational modeling, reversal learning, and dopaminergic signaling in understanding food decision processes.

topics.title: MRI Markers of Parkinsonian Nigral Degeneration
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based characterization of Parkinson disease, especially substantia nigra degeneration, iron accumulation, and microstructural changes linked to motor and multidomain dysfunction. It also includes subtype- and stage-related analyses using multiparametric and high-field imaging approaches.

keywords: the cognitive psychiatric phenotype of parkinson ’s disease through brain network dynamics
topics.title: Parkinson Disease Brain Network Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster focuses on Parkinson disease and related cognitive, psychiatric, and motor symptoms as reflected in altered brain network organization and connectivity. It emphasizes dynamic and multimodal neuroimaging biomarkers for diagnosis, subtyping, and prediction.

topics.title: OCD Networks and Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on obsessive compulsive disorder in youth, with emphasis on frontostriatal and broader brain network alterations. It also highlights clinical predictors and outcomes related to CBT and treatment response.

keywords: geometric profiling of dynamic functional network connectivity
comparative evaluation of latent modeling approaches
the reinforcement sensitivity theory latent mri systems
topics.title: Latent Modeling of Functional Network Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on comparing latent modeling approaches for analyzing dynamic functional network connectivity and related functional network structure. It also includes reinforcement sensitivity theory, psychometric scaling, and measurement invariance in MRI-based neuroanatomical studies.

topics.title: Hormonal Effects on Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how menstrual cycle-related hormones and neuroendocrine factors shape brain function, connectivity, and network balance in females. It includes work on menstrual cycle modulation, oral contraceptive discontinuation, and links to depressive disorder and major depressive disorder.

topics.title: Open Neuroimaging Data and Automation
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, standardized neuroimaging research workflows, including BIDS-based data organization, conversion, and automation. It also spans clinical and aging-related neuroimaging studies, with emphasis on datasets, visualization, and integration of physiological signals.

topics.title: Midlife Menopause and Brain Changes
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the menopause transition and related hormonal changes affect brain structure and memory in midlife women. It also includes links to hippocampal subfields, cognitive reserve, and potential risk for later-life brain pathology.

keywords: multimodal connectome wide markers of externalize behavior problems in youth
neuroanatomical subtypes of callous unemotional traits
topics.title: Youth Psychopathology and Brain Subtypes
topics.description: This cluster focuses on links between adolescent psychopathology and neurobiological variation, especially externalizing and internalizing symptom dimensions in community youth samples. It highlights multimodal brain markers, neuroanatomical subtypes, and transdiagnostic models of behavior and brain associations.

topics.title: Preterm Brain Development and Outcomes
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain development and cognitive outcomes in preterm and high-risk neonates, including effects on white matter, hippocampal development, and neonatal brain growth. It also includes related clinical contexts such as hypoxic ischaemic encephalopathy and therapeutic hypothermia.

keywords: cognitive task evoke brain body interactions
topics.title: Brain-Heart Interactions in Autonomic Control
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how brain activity relates to cardiac and cardiorespiratory signals, especially during interoception and cognitive tasks. It also includes work on autonomic regulation, heart rate variability, and multimodal ECG-fMRI approaches to characterize brain-body coupling.

keywords: brain structural connectome of adhd children
medial prefrontal glutamate and default mode network
topics.title: Neurodevelopmental connectivity in ADHD youth
topics.description: This cluster centers on ADHD and related neurodevelopmental differences in children and youth, especially structural and functional brain connectivity, maturation delay, and brain volume or white matter correlates. It also links these neural features to social responsiveness, social skills, and glutamatergic/default mode network alterations.

topics.title: Autism Connectivity and EEG Variation
topics.description: This cluster centers on autism and childhood autism, emphasizing differences in brain connectivity, functional connectivity alterations, and EEG-based markers. It also highlights clinical variation across autistic subgroups and developmental patterns in children.

keywords: fnirs evidence for consistent affective representations across viewing and recall
topics.title: Neural Processing of Facial Emotion
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain represents and responds to facial emotion, including amygdala involvement, valence and arousal coding, and face-selective network dynamics. It also includes evidence for consistent affective representations across perception and recall and broader affective predictive mechanisms.

keywords: fmri base brain function symptom integration
topics.title: Depression Subtypes and Brain Heterogeneity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on identifying clinically distinct subtypes of major depressive disorder using brain-based, multimodal, and multi-cohort approaches. It emphasizes heterogeneity in neuroanatomy and symptom associations, with links to scalable datasets and stratification methods.

topics.title: Social cognition, empathy, and neural dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how empathy, perspective taking, and social signals are supported by interacting brain networks and dynamic state changes. It also includes hyperscanning and naturalistic paradigms examining social conflict, imitation, and oxytocin-related modulation.

keywords: active gaze decodes dynamic neural states
topics.title: Subjective Processing During Naturalistic Media Viewing
topics.description: This cluster centers on how people interpret media and naturalistic stimuli, especially under uncertainty, warnings, and manipulations. It links subjective experience, attention, and neural state dynamics across viewing and perception studies.

topics.title: Machine Learning for Psychiatric MRI Classification
topics.description: This cluster focuses on machine learning and deep learning approaches for classifying psychiatric conditions from structural and multimodal MRI data. It includes schizophrenia spectrum disorder, bipolar, and unipolar depression applications, along with feature-based and interpretability methods such as morphological fingerprinting and saliency maps.

keywords: human brain dynamics change across the lifespan
individualized mapping of functional brain networks
structural brain age predicts functional connectivity
topics.title: Aging Brain Networks Across the Lifespan
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain structure and function change across adulthood and aging, with emphasis on network organization, functional connectivity, and individualized mapping. It also includes measures linking brain age and local aging patterns to cognitive and health outcomes in typical and healthy aging.

keywords: dorsal striatum activity reflect aging and inhibitory failure across the adult lifespan
topics.title: Aging, Inhibition, and Cognitive Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on how aging across the adult lifespan relates to inhibitory control, executive functions, memory, and language processing. It also includes work on neural substrates such as dorsal striatum activity and resting-state network organization.

topics.title: Multimodal Cortical Organization and Variability
topics.description: This cluster centers on how cortical and brain maps are organized across layers, regions, and modalities, with emphasis on intrinsic network structure and similarity relationships. It also includes modeling approaches for explaining multidimensional cortical variability and self-similar organization in the human cortex.

keywords: steerable multi target temporal interference stimulation
tms coil orientation modulates motor threshold estimation
uncertainty aware open source bayesian motor threshold estimation tool
topics.title: Optimizing Personalized TMS and Temporal Interference
topics.description: This cluster centers on individualized brain stimulation approaches, especially TMS montage, coil orientation, and motor mapping optimization. It also includes emerging work on temporal interference stimulation and algorithmic guidance for personalized neuromodulation.

keywords: prenatal maternal mood modulates longitudinal trajectories of brain development
prenatal maternal mood modulates longitudinal trajectories
maternal distress × cortico limbic connectivity predicts
topics.title: Perinatal Mental Health and Early Brain Development
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how maternal and parental mental health, distress, and broader perinatal adversity relate to infant and child brain development. It highlights links to neonatal functional connectivity, neural maturation, and later neurodevelopmental outcomes such as resilience and early autism risk.

keywords: dynamic developmental trajectories of emotion neural modules
emotion regulation effort predicts supplementary motor area activation
topics.title: Neural and developmental emotion regulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on the neural correlates and developmental dynamics of emotion regulation, including reappraisal, amygdala regulation, and broader emotion neural modules. It also highlights social, trait-state, and transdiagnostic factors that shape regulation capability across youth and real-world contexts.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Anxiety and Threat
topics.description: This cluster centers on how anxiety shapes threat and fear processing, with emphasis on fear conditioning, extinction, and amygdala-related responses. It also includes studies of autonomic and neural correlates of anxiety, especially in social and trait anxiety contexts.

topics.title: Neurodevelopment and resilience under adversity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how early life adversity, risk, and protective factors shape neurodevelopment and mental health during youth and adolescence. It links environmental exposures with brain structure, behavior, and longitudinal outcomes, often in high-risk populations.

keywords: the protective role of memory suppression
topics.title: Childhood Trauma, Resilience, and Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how childhood trauma and maltreatment relate to emotion regulation, resilience, and later emotional distress across mental health outcomes. It also highlights neurobiological and connectivity-based markers, including prefrontal-amygdala pathways and resting-state functional connectivity.

keywords: online closed loop transcranial focused ultrasound stimulation neuroavigation
topics.title: Transcranial Ultrasound Neuromodulation and Imaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on transcranial ultrasound stimulation methods and their integration with MRI/fMRI for targeting and validation. The abstracts emphasize closed-loop guidance, hardware development, and rapid neuroplastic effects across specific brain circuits.

keywords: coil orientation modulate tms induce changes in
personalized fc- e field guide precision tms fmri
topics.title: TMS Effects on Connectivity and Target Specificity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how transcranial magnetic stimulation modulates brain networks, functional connectivity, and BOLD responses across different protocols and stimulation targets. It also emphasizes reliability, repeatability, and factors such as coil orientation and personalized targeting that shape tms-induced changes.

keywords: midlife diet quality shapes late life brain health
topics.title: Brain aging, cognition, and reserve across adulthood
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain structure and aging-related factors relate to cognitive performance in later life. It highlights cognitive reserve, executive and memory outcomes, and multimodal MRI markers linked to brain aging and atrophy.

keywords: sex prediction use multimodal structural and functional data
topics.title: Sex Differences Across Lifespan Brain Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on sex differences in brain structure, function, and development across the lifespan, including adolescence and neurodevelopmental trajectories. It also includes multimodal approaches for sex prediction and comparative studies of anatomy, metabolism, and morphology.

topics.title: Developmental Brain Outcomes Across Childhood and Adolescence
topics.description: This cluster focuses on normative and altered brain development from infancy through adolescence, with emphasis on preterm birth, developmental trajectories, and cognitive outcomes. It connects early-life maturation patterns to later developmental variation and neuroanatomical or functional outcomes.

topics.title: Schizophrenia Network Dynamics and Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster centers on schizophrenia-related alterations in large-scale brain networks, effective connectivity, and dynamic activity patterns, especially in relation to hallucinations and network dysregulation. It also emphasizes biomarker discovery and prediction of treatment response using multimodal and dynamic measures.

keywords: predicted age deviation and abstract concept formation
brain volume base predicted age deviation
topics.title: Normative brain deviations in schizophrenia
topics.description: This cluster centers on schizophrenia and related spectrum disorders, emphasizing normative modeling to detect early deviation in brain morphology, microstructure, and structural connectivity. It also links altered brain measures such as claustrum volume and predicted age deviation to executive dysfunction and abstract concept formation.

keywords: macro structural white matter pathways alterations
topics.title: White Matter Changes in Aging and Alzheimer
topics.description: This cluster centers on white matter abnormalities across aging and the Alzheimer disease spectrum, including associations with cognitive status and disease progression. The abstracts emphasize microstructural and macrostructural alterations, especially white matter hyperintensities, as biomarkers of neurodegeneration.

topics.title: White Matter Tractography and Bundle Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on automated and development-informed tractography methods for reconstructing, segmenting, and parametrizing white matter bundles. It also includes population-level mapping, longitudinal tracking, and cross-individual matching for specific brain pathways and tractograms.

keywords: elastic net multiple kernel learning models
pronto v3.1 elastic net multiple kernel learning
topics.title: Multimodal Prediction of Memory and Brain Health
topics.description: This cluster centers on machine learning approaches for predicting memory, phenotypic outcomes, and longitudinal brain MRI measures from multimodal data. It emphasizes comparison with classical models, external validation, and foundation-model-based predictive frameworks.

topics.title: Neurodevelopment in Child Reading and Cognition
topics.description: This cluster centers on how reading-related and audiovisual tasks relate to cognitive and neural development in children. It emphasizes vocabulary, brain activation, connectivity, and developmental trajectories across early school and preschool ages.

keywords: adaptive math training drives network reorganization
development and evaluation of an intervention for adolescents
long term abacus base mental calculation learning
topics.title: Math Learning and Neural Reorganization
topics.description: This cluster centers on dyscalculia and math learning interventions, including abacus training, gamified learning, and adolescent programs aimed at improving arithmetic and word problem performance. It also emphasizes how training relates to changes in numerical networks, network integration, and cerebellar contributions.

keywords: accurate voxel wise brain age prediction
deep learning base brain age prediction
topics.title: Brain Age Prediction and Normative Modeling
topics.description: This cluster centers on estimating and interpreting brain age from neuroimaging and related signals, including deep learning and normative modeling approaches. The studies emphasize prediction accuracy, generalization, and the use of brain age gaps as markers of aging and disease-related deviation.

keywords: gamlss method for human neuroimaging case studies
multisite enigma bipolar disorder working group study
topics.title: Normative Modeling in Human Neuroimaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on normative modeling approaches for human neuroimaging, including calibration, transferability, and generalization across datasets and study settings. It also highlights applications to multisite studies and brain measures such as subcortical structure, fMRI, EEG, and DTI.

keywords: functional connectivity and genetic correlates of dimensional mood variation
topics.title: Genetic Architecture of Psychiatric Brain Traits
topics.description: This cluster centers on how genetic factors shape brain structure, connectivity, and dynamic state changes across schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, and other neuropsychiatric disorders. It emphasizes genome-wide and imaging-genetic analyses linking heritable variation to cortical and functional brain organization.

topics.title: Transdiagnostic Neuroimaging of Psychosis Subtypes
topics.description: This cluster focuses on heterogeneity and subtype structure across schizophrenia and the psychosis spectrum using transdiagnostic neuroimaging and connectivity analyses. It highlights distinct and shared neural signatures, including cortical thickness, functional connectivity, and multimodal markers linked to symptom phenotypes.

topics.title: Neural Signatures of Tinnitus
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the neural correlates and brain alterations associated with tinnitus, including chronic and somatic subtypes. It also highlights auditory, prefrontal, and neurochemical markers linked to tinnitus perception, loudness, and suppression.

topics.title: TMS for OCD and Corticostriatal Network Modulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on transcranial magnetic stimulation approaches for obsessive-compulsive disorder, especially adolescent OCD and treatment response. The abstracts emphasize clinical efficacy alongside changes in corticostriatal and prefrontal network connectivity.

keywords: functional connectivity following seizure therapy for treatment
subcallosal cingulate functional connectivity link to remission after electroconvulsive therapy
graph metrics predict electroconvulsive therapy response
topics.title: Depression Treatment Response Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression, especially treatment resistant depression, and how interventions such as electroconvulsive therapy and stanford neuromodulation therapy relate to remission and clinical response. The abstracts emphasize brain connectivity, network controllability, and plasticity as predictors or correlates of treatment outcome.

topics.title: Neural and Clinical Markers of Suicide Risk
topics.description: This cluster centers on suicide risk, suicidal ideation, and suicide attempts across depressive and bipolar disorders. It links brain imaging findings such as amygdala connectivity and hippocampal subfield volumes with social emotion recognition and related clinical trajectories.

keywords: functional connectivity correlates of observed in session rumination
personalized functional networks reveal dissociable spatial and functional topology
brain signal variability profiles associate with antidepressant treatment response
topics.title: Depression Networks, Rumination, and Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression-related brain network dysfunction, especially reward circuitry, rumination, and anterior cingulate cortex alterations. It also highlights how neural connectivity and signal variability relate to antidepressant treatment response and inflammatory influences.

keywords: multimodal neuroimaging simultaneous integration of eeg
multimodal evidence for fast optical signals in visual cortex
topics.title: Multimodal Neuroimaging and Optical Signals
topics.description: This cluster centers on simultaneous multimodal neuroimaging approaches combining EEG, MEG, fNIRS/NIRS, and OPM setups. It also includes work on fast optical signals and their multimodal evidence in visual cortex.

keywords: effective connectivity versus functional connectivity across visual cortical areas
intracortical myelin peak meg alpha beta frequencies
topics.title: Brain Connectivity and Multiscale Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on relationships between structural, functional, and effective connectivity across cortical systems, including coupling across EEG, MEG, and fMRI measures. It also highlights multiscale brain organization from intracortical myelin and gradients to stable neural fingerprints and connectomic structure.

keywords: differential network mechanisms underlying progression of subtype specific amyloid deposition
topics.title: Alzheimer Subtyping and Progression Modeling
topics.description: This cluster centers on identifying distinct Alzheimer’s disease subtypes and stages using multimodal neuroimaging and probabilistic modeling. It emphasizes biomarker progression, subtype-specific amyloid deposition, and individualized risk or phenotype inference across the Alzheimer continuum.

topics.title: Explainable MRI Deep Learning for Dementia
topics.description: This cluster focuses on applying 3D CNNs and related deep learning methods to structural brain MRI for dementia and neurodegenerative disease classification. It emphasizes explainable AI approaches, including rationale generation and Shapley-based interpretation, to improve diagnosis and prediction.

keywords: clinical translation of eeg dementia predictors
multimodal ai integrating gait analysis cfdna methylation
topics.title: Multimodal Biomarkers for Alzheimer and Dementia Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on using multimodal imaging, EEG, methylation, and machine learning approaches to predict Alzheimer disease, cognitive decline, and related dementia outcomes. It also emphasizes biomarker exploration and clinical translation across neurodegenerative disorders.

keywords: time vary scale free brain dynamic
topics.title: Naturalistic Movie Perception and Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how naturalistic movie and audiovisual stimuli are represented in the human brain, linking stimulus features and temporal structure to BOLD responses and brain encoding. It also includes work on neural variability, multifractal dynamics, and time-varying scale-free activity during naturalistic viewing.

keywords: spectral wise self supervise deep learning reconstruction
microstructure preserving diffusion mri super resolution
topics.title: Low-Field MRI Reconstruction and Diffusion Imaging
topics.description: This cluster focuses on low- and ultra-low-field MRI, especially portable and pediatric brain imaging, with an emphasis on improving image quality and accuracy. It also includes advanced reconstruction and diffusion MRI methods such as self-supervised deep learning, acceleration, and super-resolution.

topics.title: Deep Learning for MRI Segmentation
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep learning methods for automated MRI segmentation across brain tissues, lesions, and related structures. It also includes benchmarking and adaptation of segmentation pipelines for clinical and cross-domain use.

keywords: distributional tau neurodegeneration modeling reveal vulnerability resilience
topics.title: Deep Learning for Tau Progression Modeling
topics.description: This cluster centers on modeling tau PET patterns and progression in Alzheimer disease, with emphasis on individual-level prediction, regional vulnerability, and resilience. It also includes deep learning and implicit neural representation approaches for synthesizing multimodal biomarkers and generating high-resolution tau maps.

keywords: new subject level dynamic functional connectivty analysis
topics.title: Dynamic Brain Connectivity in Cognitive Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on dynamic and spatiotemporal analysis of resting-state brain activity using fMRI and EEG. The studies apply these methods to cognitive and neurodegenerative conditions including mild cognitive impairment, Alzheimer disease, frontotemporal dementia, and Parkinson disease.

keywords: ultra high b value diffusion mri
ex vivo human brainstem mri ps oct
topics.title: Ex Vivo Diffusion MRI and Optical Microstructure
topics.description: This cluster centers on diffusion MRI methods for probing brain microstructure, especially in ex vivo and postmortem tissue. It also includes optical neuroimaging and modeling approaches used to validate or complement microstructural imaging.

keywords: large multi center study diffusion mri protocols
topics.title: Multisite Diffusion MRI Reproducibility
topics.description: This cluster centers on multisite diffusion MRI studies assessing reproducibility, stability, and generalizability across scanners and protocols. It also includes quantitative analyses of white matter and fiber bundle measures such as DTI and the DTI ALPS index.

keywords: submillimeter population specific t1 weighted brain atlas
topics.title: Neuroimaging Tools and Brain Surface Analysis
topics.description: This cluster centers on neuroimaging software, atlasing, and surface-based analysis methods for brain segmentation and parcellation. It includes tools and resources for automated processing, cerebellar and hippocampal surface work, and population-specific template construction.

topics.title: Cross-Species Cortical Atlas Construction
topics.description: This cluster centers on building and refining human and nonhuman primate brain atlases, especially cytoarchitectonic and surface-based coordinate frameworks. It emphasizes cross-species mapping, template construction, and comparative alignment of cortical structure.

keywords: normative modeling of fetal cortical folding trajectories use spectral analysis of gyrification
an automatic pipeline for sulcal feature extraction
topics.title: Fetal Cortical Folding and Normative Trajectories
topics.description: This cluster focuses on modeling and characterizing cortical folding during fetal and neonatal development, with emphasis on gyrification, curvature, and trajectory-based normative analysis. It also includes atlas construction and automated sulcal feature extraction for studying folding patterns across development.

keywords: constrained dynamic functional network connectivity reveal alzheimer ’s
cross species evaluation of atomoxetine on default mode network connectivity
cross species quasi periodic patterns transferable network dynamics
recurrence networks of eeg reveal altered state space organization in alzheimer ’s disease
normative models of functional spreading network
topics.title: Dynamic Connectivity Changes in Alzheimer’s Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer’s disease and mild cognitive impairment, emphasizing heterogeneous progression, preclinical states, and alterations in default mode and broader brain network connectivity. It also includes studies of dynamic functional network organization, recurrence-based EEG analyses, and cross-species evaluations of network dynamics.

keywords: brain structural correlates of sectoral retinal nerve fibre layer thickness
sectoral retinal nerve fibre layer thickness
topics.title: Visual System Structure in Glaucoma and Retinotopy
topics.description: This cluster centers on structural and functional organization of the human visual cortex, including retinotopic mapping, V1 organization, and cortical geometry. It also includes glaucoma-related studies linking optic nerve neurodegeneration and retinal nerve fibre layer thickness to brain structural correlates.

keywords: connectome harmonics reveal alzheimer tau connectivity alignment predict cognitive decline
topics.title: Alzheimer Tau Spread and Cognitive Decline
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer disease pathology, especially tau and amyloid interactions, tau PET burden, and APOE4-related risk. It also highlights mechanisms and network-level spread linked to cognitive decline.

keywords: functional brain alterations in preclinical alzheimer 's
mtl pathology guided functional connectivity links synaptic function across
locus coeruleus connectivity confers cognitive resilience
topics.title: Preclinical Alzheimer Connectivity and Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster focuses on functional connectivity and network alterations in preclinical Alzheimer disease, especially in relation to amyloid burden, APOE risk, and cognitive decline. It also highlights biomarker and resilience-related findings, including sex-specific effects and protein signatures.

topics.title: Language Lateralization and Reorganization
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how language dominance and lateralization are organized in the brain, including typical and atypical patterns across individuals and tasks. It also examines reorganization after lesions or stroke, especially in aphasia and related language-network changes.

topics.title: Hippocampal Contributions to Memory Representation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the hippocampus supports episodic and conceptual memory, including encoding, consolidation, and representational organization across hippocampal subregions and axes. It also spans neural dynamics and memory-system interactions underlying successful and failed retrieval.

topics.title: Working Memory and Cognitive Control Networks
topics.description: These studies focus on how working memory is maintained, manipulated, and interrupted across different content domains and load conditions. They also examine the roles of prefrontal and multiple-demand networks, including DLPFC activity, in cognitive control and performance relevance.

keywords: neural correlates of episodic simulation an sdm meta
neural correlates of recast future simulations
topics.title: Neural Bases of Episodic Simulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the human brain supports episodic simulation, autobiographical memory, and related memory representations. It emphasizes neural correlates across fMRI and MEG studies, including meta-analytic work on recast future simulations and recall-related processes.

topics.title: EEG Oscillations and Cognitive Control
topics.description: This cluster centers on EEG-based signatures of cognitive state changes, especially oscillatory and aperiodic activity linked to attentional control, working memory, and time perception. It also includes predictive coding and speech-related processing, highlighting how neural dynamics support perception and task performance.

keywords: assessment of neural correlates of language processing in multilingual speakers
topics.title: Neural Basis of Multilingual Language Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain supports language processing across multilingual and bilingual speakers, including specialization, lateralization, and shared neural substrates. It also includes studies of speech, naming, and related fMRI approaches used to probe cortical language representations.

keywords: semantic information in environmental sound categories
topics.title: Neural Dynamics of Natural Language Comprehension
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the language network and related systems support naturalistic language comprehension, sentence processing, and narrative understanding. It also touches on semantic processing in sounds and the prediction of brain responses using MEG and BOLD signals.

keywords: bold asl base cerebrovascular reactivity mapping
topics.title: Cerebrovascular Reactivity and Perfusion Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on measuring cerebrovascular reactivity, cerebral blood flow, and perfusion dynamics using BOLD and ASL-based approaches. It also includes baseline physiology and normative modeling across resting-state and multi-delay perfusion imaging.

keywords: robust bold non bold ica component classification
topics.title: Multi-Echo fMRI Denoising and Classification
topics.description: This cluster centers on multi-echo fMRI processing pipelines for denoising, artifact separation, and component classification. It emphasizes ICA-based methods, distortion correction, and related preprocessing strategies for improving BOLD/non-BOLD interpretation.

keywords: hcp rs fmri pre processing pipeline
topics.title: Resting-State fMRI Reliability and Preprocessing
topics.description: This cluster centers on improving the reliability and stability of resting-state fMRI measures through preprocessing, denoising, and motion correction. It also examines how methodological choices affect functional connectivity estimates and fingerprinting performance.

keywords: cortical microarchitecture along major vascular territories
topics.title: High-Resolution Vascular MRI of Cortical Microstructure
topics.description: These studies focus on ultra-high-field MRI approaches for mapping brain vasculature and cortical microarchitecture, often at submillimeter resolution. They emphasize quantitative vessel characterization, segmentation, and vascular-territory analyses in human and animal brain tissue.

keywords: optimize retinotopic mapping for visual field coverage
topics.title: High-Field fMRI for Visual Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on ultra-high-field fMRI approaches for retinotopic and population receptive field mapping, especially in visual cortex. It emphasizes improving sensitivity, image quality, and field coverage through coil design, protocol optimization, and high-resolution acquisition.

keywords: brain metabolic networks underlying integration and disease
gradient organization of cortical metabolic connectivity
multi site multi modal mrs study
topics.title: Metabolic Brain Network Organization and Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain metabolic networks, connectomics, and metabolic gradients organize human brain function and integration. It also links MRSI and multimodal imaging approaches to disease-related metabolic signatures and mitochondrial specialization.

topics.title: Neural Representations in Vision and Alignment
topics.description: This cluster centers on how human visual and semantic representations relate to deep neural networks and fMRI-based encoding/decoding analyses. It also emphasizes representational alignment, object recognition, and the universality of neural representation across models and brains.

keywords: cortical depth profiles of mental imagery
spatiotemporal dynamics of visual mental imagery of simple and complex content
topics.title: Neural Basis of Visual Mental Imagery
topics.description: This cluster centers on how visual mental imagery is represented in the brain, including vividness, cortical activation, and individual differences such as aphantasia. It also spans topographic and spatiotemporal patterns, early visual cortex involvement, and sensorimotor contributions to imagery experiences.

keywords: cross release eeg response time decoding
multivariate bayesian inversion for trial wise decoding
topics.title: Multimodal Brain Decoding Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain decoding and classification across EEG, MEG, and fMRI, with emphasis on trial-wise decoding, Bayesian inversion, and Riemannian approaches. The abstracts also highlight cross-dataset generalization, natural speech, and data-driven integration across imaging modalities.

topics.title: Aperiodic and Oscillatory Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on characterizing aperiodic and rhythmic neural activity, especially alpha-band features, across brain states and resting-state functional connectivity. It also includes work linking these dynamics to EEG-fMRI measures and dynamic network interactions.

topics.title: Brain Dynamics, Attractors, and Network Constraints
topics.description: This cluster centers on attractor-based models of observable brain dynamics and how anatomical and effective network constraints shape cognition and neural state transitions. It also includes work on biologically constrained and reservoir-style neural models applied to large-scale human brain networks.

topics.title: White Matter Organization and Tractography
topics.description: These abstracts focus on white matter pathways, their subdivision, and their relationship to cortical and subcortical organization. They emphasize tractography-based mapping, structural connectivity, and sulcal/topological features across human and comparative brains.

keywords: bayesian non parametric serial mri harmonization
a tool for transparent and standardized assessment of multisite harmonization in neuroimaging
topics.title: Multisite Neuroimaging Harmonization Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on methods and tools for correcting site, batch, and other acquisition-related effects in neuroimaging and related data. It emphasizes harmonization frameworks, evaluation metrics, and standardized assessment across studies and modalities.

keywords: large data drive multimodal power benchmarking
network level analysis toolbox for connectome wide association study
neuromark functional networks be robust to spatial normalization template
topics.title: Robustness and reproducibility in brain network analysis
topics.description: This cluster centers on robust fMRI and connectome-wide analysis methods, emphasizing reproducibility, multiverse/robustness testing, and benchmarked network tools. It also highlights spatial normalization, variability, and template-based functional network modeling.

keywords: spectral modes of brain functional connectivity
structural connectome base functional connectivity feature selection
flexible rest state fmri network inference
topics.title: Brain Connectivity Analysis and Functional Fingerprinting
topics.description: This cluster centers on methods for analyzing brain connectivity data, including dense connectome analysis, graph-based comparison, and feature selection for functional networks. It also emphasizes robust network recovery and fingerprinting approaches linking structural and functional connectivity.

keywords: multiscale functional architecture of brain hubs
topics.title: Multiscale Brain Hub Network Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain hubs are organized and analyzed across structural and functional connectomes at multiple scales and resolutions. It emphasizes network topology, hierarchy, and directed interactions in understanding brain dynamics and information flow.

keywords: microstructural basis of structure function coupling across human resting state networks
topics.title: Structure-Function Gradients and Connectome Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on how structural and microstructural properties shape functional organization across the human brain, especially within resting-state networks and cortical gradients. It also links connectome architecture to receptor and covariance patterns underlying large-scale network specialization.

keywords: multimodal brain connectivity integration for cognitive performance prediction in youth
topics.title: Multimodal Connectivity for Cognitive Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on using functional and multimodal brain connectivity to predict individual cognitive and behavioral outcomes, including working memory, attention, and broader clinical measures. It emphasizes individualized connectome-based modeling, dynamic connectivity, and cross-subject prediction frameworks.

topics.title: Dynamic Effective Connectivity in Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on modeling and estimating dynamic and effective connectivity in brain networks, especially using fMRI and related neuroimaging data. It also includes causal inference, generative modeling, and inverse-problem approaches for understanding brain dynamics.

topics.title: Dynamic Brain States in Task fMRI
topics.description: This cluster focuses on modeling time-varying brain activity and functional connectivity during task fMRI, especially across Stroop-related paradigms. It includes probabilistic, dynamical, and neural network approaches for predicting task phase and characterizing individualized brain dynamics.
